seoTitle: How to Log Out of Claude Code Securely
metaDescription: A quick guide to logging out of Claude Code properly, so you can protect your account and sensitive project data on shared machines.
intro: Logging out of Claude Code properly matters more than it might seem, especially if you're working on a shared or client machine. This guide covers the steps to log out of Claude Code securely and why doing so protects your account and any sensitive project data. It's aimed at both individual developers and teams handling confidential work.

steps.heading: Locate the session or account controls
steps.body: Within Claude Code, find where your active session or account details are shown, since that's where the logout action lives.

steps.heading: End your session
steps.body: Trigger the logout action to close your current session and clear your active credentials from that device.

steps.heading: Confirm you're signed out
steps.body: Check that Claude Code now prompts you to sign in again, which confirms the logout was successful.

steps.heading: Log out on shared or work devices
steps.body: Make logging out a habit any time you use Claude Code on a machine you don't fully control, to keep your account and project data secure.

faq.question: Why does logging out of Claude Code matter?
faq.answer: Logging out closes your active session so someone else using the same device can't access your account or any project data you were working on.

faq.question: Will logging out delete my project history?
faq.answer: No, logging out only ends your current session — your saved projects and history remain tied to your account for when you log back in.

faq.question: Do I need to log out every time I close Claude Code?
faq.answer: On your own personal device it's optional, but on shared or work computers it's good security practice to log out each time.

faq.question: What if I forget to log out on another device?
faq.answer: Check your account's session or security settings for a way to remotely sign out of other active sessions if that option is available.
